Thread.Body:
Hello again, we again rebuild and test these units so they don't leave our facility with a C0110. Have you checked the following:

Brake Pressure Modulator Valve (BPMV) 
Electronic Brake Control Module (EBCM) Wiring
Electronic Brake Control Module (EBCM) Connector
Brake Bleeding Procedure 

If you wanted to send it back for us to retest it we can work with you on that or you can open a return request choosing "Changed my mind" for the reason and we'd have no issues working with you on a return/refund. Let us know if you have any questions. Thanks, YPS

No, the ABS pump has never worked. We continue to receive the same trouble code: C0110 (ABS circuit open). Every time this issue has occurred in the past, checking the pins that power the pump reveals incorrect voltage, which points to a faulty ABS module.

Hello again and thank you for the update.

We did see your core part number was the same as purchased. The last thing we do with these modules before they ship is hook them up to an OE level scan tool and test various functions in the module. Can you let us know if the module was installed and worked as it should for some time. Also, are they just getting no communication with the module or are there trouble codes being set by the replacement module. 

Please let us know when you get a chance. Thanks again for your help with this.

YPS
The original module was sent back to you already as there is a partial payment status pending for the core return. The original code was C0110=ABS circuit open.

Can you let us know or send us a picture of the part number sticker on your original module so we can confirm fitment and was the module calibrated to the vehicle or just plug n played? What codes are coming up? Keep us posted. Thanks, YPS

Our technician installed the module and verified that the pump shows no activity. A load test was performed on the pump circuit at the connector to the module. Battery voltage measured 11.4 V at the connector; however, the pin that powers the pump through the module only showed 6.6 V. Note that the pump itself is brand new.

Hi Bryan, we run all communication through eBay messaging for documentation purposes. Can you let us know your questions and if you have any trouble codes you can share that would be great. Can you also let us know the part number on your original module. Thanks! YPS

We just wanted to let you know we have processed your core refund today. Thanks again for your business and for participating in our core program!


Your Part Source

Hello and thank you for purchasing our rebuilt abs module!

We wanted to pass along some notes regarding install & fitment. These modules are part # or “series” specific and will “NOT” 
interchange with another part number or “series”. If you are unsure of your part number please email us a picture of the sticker on the front and we will let you know if you have purchased the correct module or point you in the right direction. If your module is off the vehicle we may ask for the number of pins in the largest connector. These units have 40 pins, but most have 11 and some have 16.

Once you have established fitment these modules need to be programmed to your vehicle with a Tech II scan tool. These units come out of many different vehicles with many different configurations, some with and some without traction control. Without programming it will not work for your vehicle. Please make sure your mechanic has the proper scan tool to do this work.

We recommend testing your abs pump especially if the vehicle is setting a C0265 C0266 C0267 or C0268 along with the wiring and wheel speed sensors prior to swapping your module as issues with these parts can set codes and/or pop the new module causing a “pump runner”.

Please check the abs fuse and replace with a properly rated fuse if blown.

Also make sure you have a scan tool capable of scanning the abs system and clear the stored codes before installation and after. It may take driving the vehicle for 15 to 20 miles for the system to reset.

We have also heard of ground point issues on these vehicles. Find the ground point (In some vehicles it is inside the driver’s door frame) and clean with a wire brush.

Email with any questions and we again thank you for your business!

YPS

My car started but it’s still has the same problem as before

Hello again,

Have you completed these instructions:

1. Disconnect battery from vehicle. Remove your original TIPM and verify you purchased the correct part number. Do not worry about the letters, only the number. If a match, tie both battery cables together “away from the battery” while you are installing the new TIPM (roughly 20 minutes). This will erase all stored codes something that needs to happen during the configuration process.
2. Install TIPM. Make sure your fuse/relay set-up is identical to the original. Check to make sure all fuses are pushed in tight. We do this but just double check us.
3. Connect battery to vehicle
5. Start vehicle and please confirm all electrical features are working properly
6. If you run into any issues please hook up an OEM level scan tool to your OBD and pull the codes from the TIPM. You’ll also need to verify your VIN is in the replacement TIPM. Once you have this info, reach out to us. A code reader cannot read the TIPM, only an OEM level scan tool. Make sure to check your ground points for any corroded or broken wires. Also, if you are having A/C issues after install please make sure fuses J15 and K9 are not popped and are pushed in. If you run into a B2206 disconnect the battery then reconnect the battery then cycle the ignition switch from off to the on position at least 5 times, leaving the ignition on for a minimum of 90 seconds per cycle.

Thanks again,
I put the new TIPM in now my car won’t start at all. Do you have any idea why this would happen?
Not a problem. Thanks again!

Thread.Body: It worked for about two months initially the problem I had was that the wipers went working after installing the part. Everything was back to normal until this week that I got a check engine light and a message saying that the oil was hot.

Hello again and thanks for contacting us with the issue, we are here to help!

We do rebuild and test these units so we have a couple quick questions so we can hopefully figure out what is going on. When you have a moment please let us know:

* Was the unit you removed from the vehicle a part number match for the one purchased? If it had a different part number let us know and we'll check our stock for a match.
* Did the Jeep run as it should for some time?
* What issues were you having with the vehicle originally
* What issues are you having with the vehicle now
* Did you make sure the fuse/relay set up in the replacement box matched your original? You may or may not need to swap a fuse or two.
* As a precaution make sure the connectors are seated.
* After trying the above if you are still having issues keep us posted. If you have an OEM level scan tool try to read the codes from the TIPM as well and let us know those. Basic code readers cannot read the TIPM unfortunately.

Keep us posted and if you have any questions at all message us back.

We appreciate your business!

YPS
Hello! I am having issues with the part purchased back in Feb. What’s the process to claim warranty?

Thanks for purchasing our TIPM, order 02-14242-68164

WE NEED YOUR VIN!! Message us back with your VIN as soon as possible or just follow the below instructions once the TIPM arrives.
 DO NOT RELY ON eBay's GUARANTEED TO FIT PROGRAM! 
 Be sure you did match the part number. As long as you do this, the TIPM is ready to configure to your vehicle. Perform the following steps to configure the TIPM:
 
1. Disconnect battery from vehicle. Remove your original TIPM and verify you purchased the correct part number. Do not worry about the letters, only the number. If a match, tie both battery cables together “away from the battery” while you are installing the new TIPM (roughly 20 minutes). This will erase all stored codes something that needs to happen during the configuration process.
2. Install TIPM. Make sure your fuse/relay set-up is identical to the original. Check to make sure all fuses are pushed in tight. We do this but just double check us.
3. Connect battery to vehicle
4. Turn key to ON position only (do not start vehicle) for 120 seconds, then turn off. If you have a “push button” start vehicle that also has a key slot, use the key slot, not the push button.
5. Start vehicle and please confirm all electrical features are working properly
 6. If you run into any issues please hook up an oem level scan tool to your OBD and pull the codes from the TIPM. You’ll also need to verify your VIN is in the replacement TIPM. Once you have this info, reach out to us. A code reader cannot read the TIPM, only an oem level scan tool. Make sure to check your ground points for any corroded or broken wires. Also, if you are having A/C issues after install please make sure fuses J15/K9 are not popped and are pushed in. If you run into a B2206 disconnect the battery then reconnect the battery then cycle the ignition switch from off to the on position at least 5 times, leaving the ignition on for a minimum of 90 seconds per cycle.

Scan Image for Installation Steps!

Thread.Body:
Hello and thanks for purchasing our repair service, order 11-14478-94733

We look forward to receiving your part, repairing it, and then sending it back to you as quickly as possible! Before you do ship it in, please let us know the following information:

-The vehicle's Year/Make/Model/Engine
-A short description of the issue.
-The diagnostic trouble codes the vehicle is sending.
-Any parts that have been repaired or replaced in an attempt to fix the issue.

Please do not ship the part to us till we have this information as unfortunately not all parts are repairable. This is the reason we ask for so much information up front as we do have a diagnostic fee of $94.99 that we would refund you down to if the unit comes in and either tests good or winds up being unrepairable. We do not want you to send it to us unless we have a good shot at a successful repair.

We will await your reply to make sure we can attempt to repair your part. Please email with any questions. Thanks again for your business, we do appreciate it!

Your Part Source
